structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit (FCU) with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Send a heating hot-water plant request if the HW valve position is greater than 95%.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Send 1 request until valve position is less than 10%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%. b. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote: a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%. b. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
evidence_text: a. If the HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 10%. b. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
judge_reason: Heating hot-water plant request rule directly quoted.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Fan Status Alarm
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan alarm is indicated by the status input being different from the output command after a period of 15 seconds after a change in output status.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Status input differs from output command for 15 seconds after a change in output status.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If commanded on, status off: Level 2 alarm. If commanded off, status on: Level 4 alarm.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: Status input does not match output command for 15 seconds after a change.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2 b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4

evidence_quote: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2 b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4
evidence_text: Fan alarm is indicated by the status being different from the command for a period of 15 seconds. a. Commanded on, status off: Level 2 b. Commanded off, status on: Level 4
judge_reason: Fan status alarm logic with timing and levels is directly supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Alarm Delay
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Wait for AlarmDelay minutes (default 30) of continuous true condition before reporting.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

evidence_quote: A fault condition that evaluates as true must do so continuously for AlarmDelay minutes before it is reported to the operator.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Test Mode
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for TestModeDelay minutes (default 120).
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

evidence_quote: Test mode shall temporarily set ModeDelay and AlarmDelay to 0 minutes for a period of TestModeDelay minutes to allow instant testing of the AFDD system and ensure normal fault detection occurs after testing is complete.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When the supply fan is proven on, fan speed and supply air temperature setpoints are controlled based on the zone state (heating, deadband, or cooling) as shown in Figure 5.20.4.3. Separate gains shall be provided for each section of the control map, or the contractor shall adjust the precise value of the x-axis thresholds to provide stable control.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: When Zone State is Heating: For heating-loop signal 100% to 50%, FANsp reset from MaxHeatSpeed to MinHeatSpeed; for 50% to 0%, FANsp is MinHeatSpeed. For heating-loop signal 100% to 50%, SATsp is Heat_SAT; for 50% to 0%, SATsp reset from Heat_SAT to active zone heating setpoint. Modulate heating coil with PID loop to maintain discharge temperature at SATsp. Cooling coil OFF. When Zone State is Deadband: FANsp shall be DeadbandSpeed; if zero, shut fan off. Cooling coil OFF. Heating coil OFF. When Zone State is Cooling: For cooling-loop signal 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed; for 50% to 100%, FANsp reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. For cooling-loop signal 0% to 50%, SATsp reset from active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT; for 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT. Modulate cooling coil with PID loop to maintain discharge temperature at SATsp. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: When the supply fan is proven on, fan speed and supply air temperature setpoints are controlled as shown in Figures 5.20.4.3. The points of transition along the x-axis shown and described are representative. Separate gains shall be provided for each section of the control map, that are determined by the contractor to provide stable control. Alternatively, the contractor shall adjust the precise value of the x-axis thresholds shown in Figure 5.20.4.3 to provide stable control.

evidence_quote: When the supply fan is proven on, fan speed and supply air temperature setpoints are controlled as shown in Figures 5.20.4.3. The points of transition along the x-axis shown and described are representative. Separate gains shall be provided for each section of the control map, that are determined by the contractor to provide stable control. Alternatively, the contractor shall adjust the precise value of the x-axis thresholds shown in Figure 5.20.4.3 to provide stable control.
evidence_text: When the supply fan is proven on, fan speed and supply air temperature setpoints are controlled as shown in Figures 5.20.4.3. The points of transition along the x-axis shown and described are representative. Separate gains shall be provided for each section of the control map, that are determined by the contractor to provide stable control. Alternatively, the contractor shall adjust the precise value of the x-axis thresholds shown in Figure 5.20.4.3 to provide stable control.
judge_reason: Clear operational sequence with specific control logic and tuning rules; well-supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Fan Speed and SAT Control in Cooling Mode
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: Supply Fan Speed and Supply Air Temperature Control
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit (FCU) with cooling coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: When Zone State is Cooling, fan speed and SAT setpoint are reset based on cooling-loop signal. The cooling coil is modulated to maintain discharge temperature at SATsp.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Cooling and there is a cooling coil.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, FANsp is reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, SATsp is reset from the active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT. The cooling coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. Heating coil OFF.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: If there is a cooling coil, when Zone State is Cooling: 1. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed. 2. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, FANsp is reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. 3. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, SATsp is reset from the active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT. 4. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT. 5. The cooling coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Heating coil OFF

evidence_quote: If there is a cooling coil, when Zone State is Cooling: 1. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed. 2. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, FANsp is reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. 3. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, SATsp is reset from the active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT. 4. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT. 5. The cooling coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Heating coil OFF
evidence_text: If there is a cooling coil, when Zone State is Cooling: 1. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, FANsp is MinCoolSpeed. 2. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, FANsp is reset from MinCoolSpeed to MaxCoolSpeed. 3. For a cooling-loop signal of 0% to 50%, SATsp is reset from the active zone cooling setpoint to Cool_SAT. 4. For a cooling-loop signal of 50% to 100%, SATsp is Cool_SAT. 5. The cooling coil shall be modulated with a PID loop to maintain the discharge temperature at SATsp. 6. Heating coil OFF
judge_reason: Detailed cooling mode control sequence fully supported by the provided evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - Operating State Definition
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The Operating State (OS) of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Determine OS per Table 5.20.6.2: OS#1 (Heating) when HC > 0 and CC = 0; OS#2 (No Heating or Cooling) when HC = 0 and CC = 0; OS#3 (Cooling) when HC = 0 and CC > 0; OS#4 (Unknown) otherwise.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The OS of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2 and Figure 5.20.6.2.

evidence_quote: The OS of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2 and Figure 5.20.6.2.
evidence_text: The OS of each FCU shall be defined by the commanded positions of the heating-coil control valve and cooling-coil control valve in accordance with Table 5.20.6.2 and Figure 5.20.6.2.
judge_reason: Operating state definition matches the quoted text.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU AFDD - SAT Too High in Full Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit (FCU) with cooling coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: If SAT is above setpoint by more than the error threshold and the cooling valve is at 99% or more, a fault condition is detected.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: SAT AVG > SATSP + ƐSAT and CC ≥ 99% for AlarmDelay minutes (default 30).
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Report a Level 3 alarm with description 'SAT too high in full cooling' and possible diagnoses including SAT sensor error, cooling coil valve stuck closed, fouled coil, CHW temperature too high, etc.
structured_payload_candidate.fault_condition: SAT AVG > SATSP + ƐSAT and CC ≥ 99%
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: FC #3 Equation SAT AVG > SAT SP + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3 Description SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling coil, CHW temperature too high or CHW unavailable, DX cooling unavailable, Gas or electric heat stuck on, Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open

evidence_quote: FC #3 Equation SAT AVG > SAT SP + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3 Description SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling coil, CHW temperature too high or CHW unavailable, DX cooling unavailable, Gas or electric heat stuck on, Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open
evidence_text: FC #3 Equation SAT AVG > SAT SP + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3 Description SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling coil, CHW temperature too high or CHW unavailable, DX cooling unavailable, Gas or electric heat stuck on, Heating coil valve leaking or stuck open
judge_reason: AFDD fault condition with equation and diagnoses is directly quoted from the guideline.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Chilled-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit (FCU) with chilled-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate chilled-water reset requests based on supply air temperature deviation and CHW valve position, provided fan is at MaxCoolSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If SAT is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests. Else if SAT is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if CHW valve position > 95%, send 1 request until valve position < 85%. Else send 0 requests.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests, c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests, d. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

evidence_quote: b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests, c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests, d. Else if the CHW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the CHW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the CHW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
evidence_text: b. If the supply air temperature is 10°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 3 requests, c. Else if the supply air temperature is 5°F greater than setpoint for 5 minutes, send 2 requests, d. judge_reason: Detailed chilled-water reset request logic fully supported by evidence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: FCU Hot-Water Reset Requests
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Fan Coil Unit (FCU) with hot-water coil
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Generate hot-water reset requests based on supply air temperature deviation and HW valve position, provided fan is at MaxHeatSpeed.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: If SAT is 17°C (30°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 3 requests. Else if SAT is 8°C (15°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 2 requests. Else if HW valve position > 95%, send 1 request until valve position < 85%. Else send 0 requests.

Else if the supply air temperature is 8°C (15°F) less than SATsp for 5 minutes, send 2 requests. d. Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

Else if HW valve position is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the HW valve position is less than 85%. e. Else if the HW valve position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.
